## Description


Manage shadcn components and projects including adding, searching, fixing, debugging, styling, and composing UI. Provides project context, component docs, and usage examples.

## When to Use

- Working with shadcn/ui components
- Adding new components to projects
- Debugging component issues
- Styling shadcn components
- Creating component compositions
- Managing component registries
- Using --preset codes

## When NOT to Use

- Non-shadcn UI frameworks
- Simple HTML/CSS
- Real-time collaborative editing
- Non-component work

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Identify Components

- Determine needed components
- Check component availability
- Review component documentation
- Plan component usage

### Phase 2: Add Components

- Initialize shadcn project if needed
- Add components to registry
- Configure component settings
- Verify installation

### Phase 3: Implement & Style

- Use components in code
- Apply custom styling
- Compose components together
- Test functionality

### Phase 4: Debug & Optimize

- Fix component issues
- Optimize performance
- Ensure accessibility
- Document usage

## Tools & References

- **Related Skills**: frontend-design
- **shadcn/ui**: https://ui.shadcn.com
- **Components**: Registry of available components
- **Presets**: --preset codes for quick setup

## Best Practices

- Use consistent component styling
- Follow shadcn conventions
- Test components thoroughly
- Document custom modifications
- Keep components accessible
- Update components regularly
- Share component patterns with team
